summary: Parse `[requirement.<name>]` (`means`, `required`) in `temper.toml` ÔÇö the meaningful-contract namespace, parse-only

per.section: Requirements and `satisfies` ÔÇö the meaningful contract

edit.description: Add a `Requirement { means: String, required: bool }` struct and a `requirements: BTreeMap<String, Requirement>` field on `AuthorLayer`, parsed from top-level `[requirement.<name>]` tables in `AuthorLayer::parse` ÔÇö its own namespace, distinct from `kind`/`role` (`specs/10-contracts.md`, the Decision's closing note: "`requirement.` is its own namespace ÔÇö distinct from the `rule` artifact kind"). `means` is a required string carried verbatim and NEVER interpreted (law 3 ÔÇö no proxy); `required` is an optional bool (absent Ôçĺ false: temper never fabricates a gate the author did not declare, `00-intent.md` law 4). Mirror the `[role.<name>]` parse path: a `requirement` root that is not a table, a `[requirement.<name>]` that is not a table, and a missing/mistyped `means` are each a load error (new `ComposeError` variants: `RequirementRootNotTable`, `RequirementNotTable`, `RequirementMissingMeans`, `RequirementWrongType`). Add a `requirements(&self) -> &BTreeMap<String, Requirement>` accessor. Parse-only in this tier: coverage over the parsed requirements is a follow-on entry (REQUIREMENT-COVERAGE).

Extend the acceptance suite with `AuthorLayer::parse` cases over the new namespace: a `[requirement.dev-standards]` with `means` + `required = true` parses to the typed `Requirement`; `means` is stored verbatim (never judged); an absent `required` defaults to false; a `[requirement.x]` missing `means`, or a non-table `requirement` root, is a load error. Import the new `Requirement` type from `temper::compose`.

tests.asserts: `[requirement.<name>]` parses into a typed Requirement (means verbatim, required defaulting false); a missing `means` or malformed `requirement` table is a ComposeError load error.

acceptance: `AuthorLayer` carries a name-keyed `requirements` map parsed from `[requirement.<name>]` tables (`means` required + verbatim, `required` optional), exposed via `requirements()`; malformed declarations are load errors; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. No coverage check yet (that is REQUIREMENT-COVERAGE).
notes: Foundation for the reframe (inbox, spec dacec45): a requirement carries meaning, not predicates ÔÇö temper NEVER interprets `means` (law 3). Parse mirrors the existing `[role.<name>]` / `[kind.<name>]` paths already in compose.rs, so no new mechanism, just a new namespace + typed value. Disjoint from the representation + coverage entries that chain behind it.

summary: Carry `satisfies` + `rationale` on the artifact representation ÔÇö extend the skill/rule IR + features so meta.toml holds them, round-tripped

per.section: Each artifact directory is a representation, not a copy

edit.description: Add representation-level fields to the `Skill` IR: `satisfies: Vec<String>` (the requirements this artifact opts into filling ÔÇö the coverage-check binding) and `rationale: Option<String>` (the authored *why*, `00-intent.md` law 7). These are AUTHORED on the surface, NOT frontmatter that round-trips to the source `.claude/` file ÔÇö so they live under a dedicated `[representation]` table in `meta.toml`, kept out of the frontmatter fields the contract engine ranges over. `from_source_dir` sets both empty/None (the source carries neither); `to_meta_document` emits `[representation]` with `satisfies`/`rationale` only when non-empty (so an unauthored skill's meta.toml is byte-identical to today ÔÇö import idempotence holds); `from_surface_dir` reads them back and must skip `representation` when rebuilding `extra` (as it already skips `provenance`). Add in-module round-trip tests.

Mirror the skill change on the `Rule` IR: `satisfies: Vec<String>` + `rationale: Option<String>` under a `[representation]` table in `meta.toml`, authored-not-imported, kept out of the frontmatter `extra` (skip `representation` alongside `provenance` in `from_surface_dir` and `KNOWN_KEYS` handling). `from_source_file` sets both empty/None; `to_meta_document` emits the table only when non-empty (a no-frontmatter rule with no representation stays byte-identical). Add in-module round-trip tests proving satisfies/rationale survive the surface round-trip and never leak into the reconstructed frontmatter.

Add `satisfies: Vec<String>` to `Features` (a representation binding the coverage check reads, distinct from the frontmatter `fields` map ÔÇö a `satisfies` link is NOT a contract-checkable field, it is the referential edge coverage resolves). Populate it in `skill_features` (from `skill.satisfies`) and `rule_features` (from `rule.satisfies`). `rationale` is carried in the IR but NOT extracted ÔÇö it is the human *why*, never a decidable feature. Update the in-module tests to assert `Features.satisfies` reflects the IR.

tests.asserts: A skill authored with satisfies/rationale round-trips through the surface (meta.toml `[representation]` Ôćĺ from_surface_dir) identically, and neither leaks into the frontmatter fields; an unauthored skill's meta.toml is unchanged.
skill_features/rule_features expose the IR's `satisfies` on Features.satisfies, and it stays out of the frontmatter `fields` map.

acceptance: The skill and rule IRs carry authored `satisfies` + `rationale` under a `[representation]` meta.toml table, round-tripped byte-faithfully and kept out of the contract-checked frontmatter; `Features` exposes `satisfies` for the coverage check; import stays idempotent; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The representation is an authoring space, not a lint target (`specs/20-surface.md`): header/satisfies/rationale authored, body carried, conformance derived. Scoped to the built-in skill+rule kinds; custom-kind Unit satisfies is a later follow-on (noted, not silently cut). Disjoint files from REQUIREMENTS-PARSE (compose.rs) and REQUIREMENT-COVERAGE (main/lib/coverage.rs); chained after parse so the coverage entry has one landed upstream.

summary: `check` gates referential coverage ÔÇö every `required` requirement has a resolving `satisfies`; diagnose unfilled requirements and dangling links

per.section: Requirements and `satisfies` ÔÇö the meaningful contract

new.description: The referential COVERAGE check (`specs/10-contracts.md`, "What `check` gates is the decidable shadow"): `pub fn check(requirements: &BTreeMap<String, Requirement>, artifacts: &[Features]) -> Vec<Diagnostic>`. Two decidable, true-positive diagnostics ÔÇö (1) UNFILLED: every `required` requirement is satisfied by Ôëą1 artifact whose representation declares a resolving `satisfies` link naming it; a required requirement with no such artifact is an `error` finding. (2) DANGLING: every `satisfies` entry on any artifact names a declared requirement; a `satisfies` resolving to no requirement is an `error` finding on that artifact. This is the referential primitive ÔÇö decidable coverage, a true positive every time ÔÇö and temper NEVER judges whether the artifact *actually* fulfils `means` (that is the author's attestation, optionally a wired `verified_by`). Rule ids like `requirement.unfilled` / `requirement.dangling`.

Fixture-driven proof: a `required` requirement with a skill declaring a resolving `satisfies` stays silent; a `required` requirement with no satisfying artifact fires UNFILLED; a skill whose `satisfies` names no declared requirement fires DANGLING; a non-`required` requirement left unfilled does not block. An end-to-end case over the built binary (mirroring tests/temper_toml.rs) proving `temper check` exits non-zero on an unfilled required requirement and zero when covered.

edit.description: Add `pub mod coverage;` beside the other engine modules (this entry owns the new module's `pub mod` line, so no other entry co-edits lib.rs).

Wire coverage into `gate()`, inside the existing `if let Some(layer)` block (requirements only exist with a `temper.toml`): assemble all artifact features (skill_features chained with rule_features), then `diagnostics.extend(coverage::check(layer.requirements(), &all_features))`. Coverage findings are `error` severity (a `required` requirement unfilled, or a dangling `satisfies`, blocks the gate) ÔÇö the gate stays closed. Absent `temper.toml` Ôçĺ no layer Ôçĺ no requirements Ôçĺ this adds nothing, so the floor-only path is byte-for-byte unchanged.

tests.asserts: Unfilled required requirement and dangling satisfies each fire an error; a covered required requirement + resolving satisfies stays silent; a non-required unfilled requirement does not block; `temper check` exit code reflects the verdict.

acceptance: `temper check` gates referential coverage ÔÇö every `required` requirement resolves to Ôëą1 artifact's `satisfies`, and every `satisfies` resolves to a declared requirement ÔÇö emitting precise unfilled/dangling diagnostics and exiting non-zero on a required violation; `means` is never judged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Closes the reframe: the contract reads as *intent* ("the harness must maintain dev standards") while the gate stays *weak* ("does that intent have a resolving home?") ÔÇö meaningful contract, weak gate (law 3). Ranges over skill+rule features (SATISFIES-REPRESENTATION's scope). Disjoint files (main/lib/coverage) from the two upstream entries; chained last so both upstreams have landed.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind ÔÇö `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR ÔÇö the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra` (never dropped), a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }` (SHA-256 of the source bytes). Ship `from_source_file` (parse + hash), `to_meta_document` (`toml_edit` header, comment/order preserving), and `from_surface_dir` (reload the written surface). Round-trip discipline: body copied byte-for-byte, only the header rewritten (`.claude/rules/rust.md`). The name is the file stem; identity is the path, not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml` ÔÇö decidable clauses only, identity by path not name. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (marked harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic/undecidable clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3) ÔÇö extraction mirrors the real subagent format, the contract is a layerable template (`specs/40-composition.md`).

Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one deliberately-broken agent per clause proving it fires (missing `description`, an inert `globs` key, an uppercase `name`) and a clean agent that trips nothing; an `insta` import round-trip proving re-importing an unchanged agent yields an identical surface (idempotence).

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this foundation entry owns the `pub mod` line so no other entry co-edits `lib.rs`).

Extend `Workspace` with an `agents: Vec<Agent>` field and load it under `<dir>/agents/*` in `Workspace::load` (name-sorted via the existing `surface_dirs`), and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the in-module load tests to construct/assert the new field.

Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as `string` scalars, every `extra` key folded into the same name-keyed map so `forbidden_keys` resolves the ignored Cursor keys, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}` via the Agent IR, and add a `[[agent]]` row per agent to the `lock.toml` roll-up ÔÇö parallel to the existing rule discovery/import/lock path. Extend the import idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into the shared `gate()`: add a `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT` embed, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective agent contract (floor ÔŐĽ layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to the `by_kind` map, and add the agent floor to the `schema` command's `floors` vec.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` artifacts to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred under the requirements/satisfies reframe (inbox, spec dacec45): more built-in kinds is the wrong direction ÔÇö the value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind. Files/acceptance stay valid for that revival; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. If revived, re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
